name: Leland F Harris Inc
principals: Patsy Harris (runs business); Larry B Harris (CEO/listed exec); founded by Leland F. Harris (d. 2010)
districts_or_customers: Corning-Painted Post Area School District (one of four local contractors alongside Birnie, Loren Smith, Titus)
recent_news: Founder Leland F. Harris died 2010; he was 1991 School Bus Contractor of the Year and former president of the School Bus Contractors Association. Business has continued under family ownership since.
fit_notes: Classic small family-owned NY school bus contractor in Summit footprint with multi-generational ownership and clear succession dynamics (founder deceased 2010, second-generation now in 60s+). Long-tenured Corning-Painted Post district relationship is attractive, though fleet is likely small (<15 buses).

name: Berlington Transporting
districts_or_customers: Likely Benton Area SD or Columbia/Luzerne County districts in PA (not confirmed online)
recent_news: No public news or web presence located; minimal digital footprint typical of very small rural PA contractor.
fit_notes: Small rural Benton, PA owner-operator with sub-$500K revenue and minimal online footprint. Too small to be a meaningful standalone tuck-in but could fit as a bolt-on if Summit is consolidating northeastern PA route density. Owner-name eponymous business suggests succession potential.

name: Salden School Bus Svc
principals: Ed Salden (President); Frank Salden (earlier generation/founder, documented as bus driver in 1952 press photo)
districts_or_customers: Likely Eastern Carver County Schools (District 112) area and/or St. John's Lutheran School, Chaska MN
recent_news: No recent M&A news; multi-generational Salden family operation with documented presence in Chaska school transportation since at least 1952.
fit_notes: Multi-generational MN family operator (70+ year history) in growing Twin Cities west exurb (Chaska). Strong succession signal given the Salden surname spanning 1950s to today. MN is an expansion state per record but the deep local incumbency and Carver County growth corridor make this an attractive small tuck-in or relationship platform.

name: Battle Buddy Mobility Trnsprt
principals: Kevin L. Hedrington (Owner, service-disabled veteran); Stephanie Hedrington (family member on team)
fleet_size_est: small NEMT van fleet (wheelchair-accessible, bariatric-capable); fleet size not disclosed
employees_est: ~8-40 (about 8-9 named staff on site; record lists 40)
service_types: NEMT; non-emergency medical transportation; some school transport listed among customer categories; wheelchair/special-needs
districts_or_customers: VA (Department of Veterans Affairs contract); hospitals, dialysis centers, schools, churches, general public in Battle Creek MI region
recent_news: Founded 2021. SBA-certified Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB). Pending NEMTAC accreditation. Certified veteran-friendly employer by Michigan Veterans Affairs Agency.
fit_notes: Poor fit for Summit. Primarily NEMT (not a yellow-bus operator); record's 'special_needs' flag reflects wheelchair-accessible NEMT, not SPED school routes. Founded 2021 with no significant K-12 district contracts identified, and SDVOSB designation is valuable to the owner (set-aside contracts) making sale unlikely. Skip.

name: Elm City Livery Inc
principals: 'Bill Scalzi (Owner)' per source record, but public sources tie Bill Scalzi to Metro Taxi of West Haven (founded 1987, ~161 cabs); Scalzi link to Elm City Livery is unverified and may reflect common-ownership or record error
fleet_size_est: unknown; livery fleet (sedans/vans), not yellow buses
employees_est: ~300 (per record; consistent with combined Metro Taxi/livery group)
service_types: livery; non-emergency/disability transportation; not a school-bus contractor
districts_or_customers: General public; disability transport; appears on CT DOT general livery permit holders list
recent_news: No M&A news identified. Bill Scalzi's Metro Taxi of West Haven (same address area) is a well-known CT taxi/livery operator; relationship to Elm City Livery is unclear from public sources.
fit_notes: Likely NOT a school-bus tuck-in target. Public records describe Elm City Livery as a livery / transportation-for-the-disabled operator on the CT DOT general livery permit list, not a yellow-bus contractor. Possibly affiliated with Bill Scalzi's Metro Taxi group. Unless Summit is pursuing CT SPED/NEMT diversification, recommend deprioritizing.
